verdict_detail: Frozen 96^3 3-warmup/5-sample/4-round qualification passed end to end under the corrected flux/observer convention (commit 1cc4a71: Yee-staggered E-plain/H-retard observer DFT and full-primal NF2FF quadrature). The physical power closure |P_accepted - P_rad| / P_incident is 6.971e-4 (0.0697%), well inside the 1% frozen gate, restoring the recorded commit 3223e0c value of 6.997e-4 (0.07%) after the plain-plain-convention regression that measured 2.865% on 4b24b60. All numerical gates pass with wide margin. The timing loop was reached and passed: basis+16-combine is 20.55% of 16 direct solves (gate <= 40%) and one combine is 0.0343% of one direct solve (gate < 10%). Combining beams executes zero additional FDTD steps (combine is a pure einsum). The 3/5/4 protocol is exact, so this is a qualifying run.

protocol.note: Full frozen protocol executed: numerical gates asserted first, then the timing loop.

host.current: 2x NVIDIA RTX A6000 (50899386368 bytes), driver 595.71.05, torch 2.13.0+cu130, CUDA 13.0, run on one GPU (CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0), numactl --cpunodebind=0 --membind=0, governor performance, no competing compute processes
host.previous_recorded: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 (16303 MiB), driver 596.49, torch 2.10, CUDA 12.8
host.re_anchor_reason: The frozen performance contract host is re-anchored from the retired RTX 5080 to this 2x A6000 host per the plan re-qualification host rule. The re-anchor is authorized because this qualification passed every gate, including the physical power-closure gate that failed under the since-corrected plain-plain observer convention. AcceptanceBudget.local_hardware is updated to name this host.
